pr_title: DOM-75309 Add taxonomy tags to list apps public API

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body:
suggestion (non-blocking): The flag check runs even when there are zero apps. 
Maybe move both the flag check and the taxonomy call inside the non-empty branch to avoid the flag lookup

comments.author: ddl-aj-rossman
comments.body: good call 👍

pr_title: DOM-75309 Add taxonomy tags to list apps public API

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body:
question: The schema says id, label, and namespaceId as required, but here None gets mapped to "". Could this be confusing to API consumers that could be expecting values given those are required fields?
I assume that's for backwards compatibility of some kind?

comments.author: ddl-aj-rossman
comments.body: Good catch: it's just defensive handling of the generated client model which types these as Option[String]. In practice, all of these fields should be present

pr_title: DOM-75309 Add taxonomy tags to list apps public API

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body:
suggestion: Nice coverage!!
One case might be missing, if I read things right. 
In `getTagsForEntities`, it can throw but we handle it as `NonFatal`. 
Maybe it would be good to verify the controller still returns apps with `taxonomyTags = None` in such cases.

pr_title: [DOM-75531] Support reproducibility of imported projects and repos for app versions

comments.author: adrianrsy
comments.body: Main relevant caller sets this value already.

pr_title: [DOM-75531] Support reproducibility of imported projects and repos for app versions

comments.author: adrianrsy
comments.body: Done

pr_title: [DOM-75531] Support reproducibility of imported projects and repos for app versions

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body:
question: If I read this right, setting these to None means any new app version will immediately fail.
Do we need to keep the distinction `None = legacy` X `Some(empty) = "no repos"` here?
Or make these `Some(Seq.empty)`, to be safe, and let the copier resolve it later.

comments.author: adrianrsy
comments.body:
This app version created by ProjectForkerAndCopier is already non-fuctional due to the main commit fields being set to None.
Considering the migration which should handle the majority of legacy cases, None represents "we don't have reproducibility information so we won't allow this to be reproduced".

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body: note: in a rare case where the branch/repo has no commits, this would return null. If easy, we should probably try to handle this, just in case this method gets used for other more general purpose

pr_title: [DOM-75531] Support reproducibility of imported projects and repos for app versions

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: what is a `release`? is this better/more correct than just do the `filesService.getHeadCommitIdForProject()` below? this feels weird that we're getting the resolved commit from a `run`

comments.author: adrianrsy
comments.body: Similar to a tag in git if I'm not mistaken but explicitly tied to a run. This should be the correct way to get the commit if the imported project settings specify a release rather than head or a commit.

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body:
still feels weird -- why is a "run" the source of truth for a release?

but i'll trust that this is the right way

pr_title: [DOM-75531] Support reproducibility of imported projects and repos for app versions

comments.body: question: I assume we'll rely on migration to always have `resolvedCommitId` for existing apps?

comments.author: adrianrsy
comments.body: Yes.

pr_title: [DOM-75531] Support reproducibility of imported projects and repos for app versions

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body: nice documentation!

pr_title: [DOM-75531] Support reproducibility of imported projects and repos for app versions

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body:
my other comment about the docs was more high level. in general i feel like docstrings on classes or methods in a `/PROJECT/INTERFACE` kind of directory -- like `/apps/interface` -- should not talk about low level implementation that depends on _other_ projects. `/apps/interface` technically has _no idea_ about runs etc. It's only the monolith-adapter that knows about things like `ExecutionSpecificationBuilder` etc.

so im not convinced this is the best place for these docs, but im fine with it if you can't think of a better place that is closer to the point in the code where all this stuff comes together.

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: and i really do mean im fine with it! i just wanted to make the more general point clearer

comments.author: adrianrsy
comments.body:
Closer to the execution layer of things would be nice, but the edge cases are only relevant to apps for now and I'm not sure we have a good enough reproducibility story for dependencies across all execution types to make any comments generic to all of them.

I think I'll leave it in here for now.

pr_title: DOM-75386 Preserve route and query params in extended identity propagation consent callback

comments.author: ddl-eric-jin
comments.body: I think we should also check that the query parameters are added as expected.

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body:
that's not possible here. this is a unit test spec for the execution resource params maker. the query params and route come from an actual app request via `your browser => ... some hops ... => app nginx lua code`

i already linked it to the e2e test ticket and the AC there, it's something we can and should test there. https://dominodatalab.atlassian.net/browse/DOM-75049

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: oh, and if we had "integration" tests for apps, we could test it there -- so best we can do is an e2e test

pr_title: [DOM-75403] Cleanup n+1 queries in Apps

comments.author: adrianrsy
comments.body: Does not need a fix. The canReadUsageStatistics gate only exists on the dedicated single-app usage statistics endpoint (getTotalViews), not on the summary view counts shown in the listing page. Our batch refactoring preserved this existing behavior exactly.

pr_title: DOM-75428 Add tag ID filtering to listApps API

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body:
question: is it ok to just return empty here if none of the tags match? 
It seems, functionally, correct to me, it's just that the warning log message suggests that might not be always expected.

comments.author: ddl-aj-rossman
comments.body: IMO I think we should keep this log since this case happens when there is a taxonomy service failure, not if none of the tags match. If the call succeeds but returns an empty list of entityIds, then this error won't be logged

pr_title: DOM-75428 Add tag ID filtering to listApps API

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body:
question: any concerns with this being a large list of `allowedAppIds` that could make this problematic Unlike the other things in this filter, I assume a search coming from tags could return a number of ids.

Honestly I wouldn't be too worried if this is in the dozens but I also don't know how large a list of params we can pass to mongo (I'm assuming that's what this does).

CC: @ddl-ryan-connor

comments.author: ddl-aj-rossman
comments.body:
Good question- a few points:
- generally, Mongo's `$in` can handle large lists. There is a 16 MiB limit for the size of the query document, but even with thousands of AppIDs, we won't approach it
- Performance wise, we should be ok since we're querying on the app's ID, which is indexed.
